name: Thurgau (Switzerland)
description: A square flag divided diagonally, white over green, with two gold lions passant along the division.
history: An 1803 design based on the two lions of the House of Kyburg, which ruled Thurgau in the 13th century; green and white were 'revolutionary' colours of the cantons newly created from former subject territories.

features.symbolism: a diagonal division of white over green

features.role: two lions passant along the bend
features.symbolism: from the arms of the House of Kyburg
